name: Thanksgiving Turkey Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the turkey slices to about 1/3 to 1/2 inch thick (about 12 ounces).
2. Place a slice of turkey between two sheets of wax paper and pound it.
3. Mound about 1/4 cup of stuffing into the center of each slice and roll up.
4. Dust the rolls with flour on both sides.
5. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
6. Add the turkey rolls, open ends facing up, and brown all sides for about 5 minutes (some stuffing may spill out).
7. Remove the turkey rolls from the skillet and add Madeira. Scrape up any browned pieces from the bottom of the pan.
8. Add chicken broth and return the turkey rolls to the pan. Cover and cook over low heat until cooked through, about 7 to 10 minutes.
9. Serve with gravy and any remaining stuffing in the skillet.
STUFFING INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Heat butter in a large skillet.
2. Add shallot and garlic, sautéing until tender for about 5 minutes.
3. Add sausage (ground pork), breaking it up with a fork, and cook until browned.
4. Add sage, red pepper flakes, and thyme.
5. Crumble the bread and add it to the mixture.
6. Cook over low heat for 5 minutes to blend flavors.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
